title: flake: Error adding network: failed to list chains
body:
Flake: seen only once, on rawhide gating tests (cgroups v2), cannot reproduce:
```
not ok 17 podman run --pull
# (from function `die' in file ./helpers.bash, line 263,
#  from function `run_podman' in file ./helpers.bash, line 164,
#  in test file ./030-run.bats, line 122)
#   `run_podman run --pull=never $IMAGE true' failed with status 126
# $ /usr/bin/podman rm --all --force
# $ /usr/bin/podman images --all --format {{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}} {{.ID}}
# quay.io/libpod/alpine_labels:latest 4fab981df737
# $ /usr/bin/podman run --pull=missing quay.io/libpod/alpine_labels:latest true
# $ /usr/bin/podman run --pull=never quay.io/libpod/alpine_labels:latest true
# time="2020-05-14T12:08:02-04:00" level=error msg="Error adding network: failed to list chains: running [/usr/sbin/iptables -t nat -S --wait]: exit status -1: "
# time="2020-05-14T12:08:02-04:00" level=error msg="Error while adding pod to CNI network \"podman\": failed to list chains: running [/usr/sbin/iptables -t nat -S --wait]: exit status -1: "
# Error: error configuring network namespace for container 8442fe4a528a15c65a8cd54707246cab846661d5ef7d831cc40c2a4af5b8bbd2: failed to list chains: running [/usr/sbin/iptables -t nat -S --wait]: exit status -1:
# [ rc=126 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
# #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
# #| FAIL: exit code is 126; expected 0
# #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
```
podman-2.0.0-0.24.dev.git7e9ed37.fc33
containernetworking-plugins-0.8.5-7.1.gitf7a2fc9.fc33
iptables-nft-1.8.4-7.fc33

Does not seem to be #5352:
```console
# grep back /etc/cni/net.d/87-podman-bridge.conflist
```

Seems very closely related to #5569, except mine is on Fedora Rawhide and not easily reproduced.

This is an on-demand VM, spun up solely for running gating tests. It has no other activity on it.

comments.body:
Another failure (still no reproducer, just a brute-force series of reruns):
```
✗ podman run - basic tests
   (from function `die' in file /usr/share/podman/test/system/helpers.bash, line 263,
    from function `run_podman' in file /usr/share/podman/test/system/helpers.bash, line 164,
    in test file /usr/share/podman/test/system/030-run.bats, line 47)
     `run_podman $expected_rc run $IMAGE "$@" </dev/null' failed
   $ podman rm --all --force
   $ podman images --all --format {{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}} {{.ID}}
   quay.io/libpod/alpine_labels:latest 4fab981df737
   $ podman info --format {{ .Host.OCIRuntime.Path }}
   /usr/bin/crun
   13
   $ podman run quay.io/libpod/alpine_labels:latest true
   $ podman run quay.io/libpod/alpine_labels:latest false
   [ rc=1 (expected) ]
   $ podman run quay.io/libpod/alpine_labels:latest sh -c exit 32
   [ rc=32 (expected) ]
   $ podman run quay.io/libpod/alpine_labels:latest echo DdmqIO4zTg5Yw0v6EZhIfohj13f4tI
   time="2020-05-14T13:12:43-04:00" level=error msg="Error adding network: failed to list chains: running [/usr/sbin/iptables -t nat -S --wait]: exit status -1: "
   time="2020-05-14T13:12:43-04:00" level=error msg="Error while adding pod to CNI network \"podman\": failed to list chains: running [/usr/sbin/iptables -t nat -S --wait]: exit status -1: "
   Error: error configuring network namespace for container c3207d69c0e33e22e75a5165dc21850bcd24f4d6ec0b0f174550f9387b045df0: failed to list chains: running [/usr/sbin/iptables -t nat -S --wait]: exit status -1:
   [ rc=126 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
```
This is a simple `podman run`, without `--pull` as in the first report.

Oh hi, and another one on my immediate retry:
```
 ✗ podman run - basic tests
   (from function `die' in file /usr/share/podman/test/system/helpers.bash, line 263,
    from function `run_podman' in file /usr/share/podman/test/system/helpers.bash, line 164,
    in test file /usr/share/podman/test/system/030-run.bats, line 47)
     `run_podman $expected_rc run $IMAGE "$@" </dev/null' failed with status 126
   $ podman rm --all --force
   $ podman images --all --format {{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}} {{.ID}}
   quay.io/libpod/alpine_labels:latest 4fab981df737
   $ podman info --format {{ .Host.OCIRuntime.Path }}
   /usr/bin/crun
   13
   $ podman run quay.io/libpod/alpine_labels:latest true
   $ podman run quay.io/libpod/alpine_labels:latest false
   time="2020-05-14T13:17:59-04:00" level=error msg="Error adding network: failed to list chains: running [/usr/sbin/iptables -t nat -S --wait]: exit status -1: "
   time="2020-05-14T13:17:59-04:00" level=error msg="Error while adding pod to CNI network \"podman\": failed to list chains: running [/usr/sbin/iptables -t nat -S --wait]: exit status -1: "
   Error: error configuring network namespace for container 7d1917af479e0c1865b6e800306ccf8ac88754b06b9942f6546ceb93235d13b2: failed to list chains: running [/usr/sbin/iptables -t nat -S --wait]: exit status -1:
   [ rc=126 (** EXPECTED 1 **) ]
```
This one is on `run false`.

I still can't get a simple one-command reproducer, but I am comfortable recommending this:
```bash
# while bats /usr/share/podman/test/system/030-run.bats; do :; done
```
It consistently fails for me within a few iterations.
Exit status 1 is thoroughly unhelpful, but I somewhat doubt that there's more detailed output available, knowing CNI.
@edsantiago Have we seen this one recently?
Oooh! We actually [have data now!](http://file.bos.redhat.com/~santiago/cirrus-flake-xref.html). No, I don't see it. Closing, will reopen if it recurs.
